data.body: CLAIM PHASE ~COMPLETE: 10/11 claimed (epic + ac0e,e1f2,a37b,f32e,24ec,a8e5,b9c0,74d9,9f25 all in_progress). Only d4cf open (rv6 running). Per operator root-cause guidance, all blocks were valid and remediated honestly (NO force used). Convergence came from CODE-GROUNDED corrections, not iteration-count: a8e5 passed once I corrected the detector identity (P5 already emits subjects, no P6 sibling-overlap detector); 74d9 passed once I dropped the invented scorer_args and read the annotation from the case dict directly (verified vs eval_scorers.py) + made the eval confirmatory-not-close-gating; 9f25 passed once validity/impact/priority were sourced DIRECTLY from the sidecar's pass3 decision fields (decide.py:336-338) instead of an unstated source; b9c0 passed at the DEFINITE-minimal 'security-only' scope + all 3 pinning tests. d4cf: the big win was abandoning the over-engineered parent_plan context-plumbing for the ticket's ORIGINAL intent — G7 as an AGENT criterion reads the parent via its always-available show_ticket tool (pai_tools.py) at runtime; and registering it AGENT_TIER-not-CODEBASE_GROUNDED (tooling is granted by agentic execution_mode per registry.py:62-65, not by CODEBASE_GROUNDED). HEAD still frozen 0fb8d17e6. Next: claim d4cf, then held-out-TDD implementation of the stack.
